key_insight: Mask-aware policy gradients improve masked diffusion language models by treating generation as two coupled decisions: predicting tokens and choosing which positions to remask.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical reinforcement-learning recipe for training MDLMs on reasoning tasks where standard likelihood-based optimization is intractable or incomplete.
method: Formulate MDLM generation as a two-stage action MDP.
Decompose the policy gradient into a token-prediction term and a masking/remasking term.
Optimize both terms jointly instead of only token predictions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports state-of-the-art results on mathematical reasoning and coding benchmarks, including 87.1% on GSM8K and 53.4% on MBPP.
watch_item: The masking term adds optimization complexity, so implementation must carefully model remasking decisions and avoid unstable reward estimation.
paper_title: Mask-Aware Policy Gradients for Diffusion Language Models
